what_it_does: Creates the shared channel substrate in Supabase: public.channels (id/kind/name/purpose/mode/coordinator/status/members jsonb/origin jsonb/posts count/shared bool/timestamps) and public.channel_posts (uuid id, channel_id FK cascade, from_session, sender_kind, to_session, thread, kind, text, created_at) with three indexes. Adds a trigger function _bump_channel_on_post that recounts posts and updates last_activity on each insert, registers both tables in the supabase_realtime publication, enables RLS, sets grants (service_role ALL, authenticated SELECT, no anon), and ensures the pgcrypto extension for gen_random_uuid().
observations: channels mirrors the existing agent_sessions/channels.jsonl shape; channel_posts mirrors the mail-leaf post shape (frm/to/thread/text/ts) — stated as the alignment for fork's company-fabric → Supabase sync
Header marks the schema PROVISIONAL pending fork's confirm/adjust
shared boolean is OPT-IN visibility, fail-closed: false=internal (never leaves the box), true=publishes + client-eligible; per-client scoping layered on top via clients.channels
sender_kind ('session'|'client') tags origin so the company injector skips self-origin posts and tags Claude Design posts
posts is a denormalised count maintained by the after-insert trigger (full recount per insert)
Realtime publication add wrapped in a DO block catching duplicate_table so the migration is re-runnable
RLS enabled but NO policies defined here — comment defers them to 0005/0006; explicitly no anon grant and no broad using(true) policy (C-1: would OR-widen past scoped policies)
Participation model described as Edge Function write → Realtime → company session reply → Realtime back to Claude Design
standouts: Supabase-as-neutral-room, zero-box-exposure architecture (both sides reach OUTBOUND to Supabase; nobody connects into Tim's machine)
Two-level fail-closed visibility: shared=true AND client-listed
The C-1 caution: avoid a broad using(true) anon/SELECT policy that would OR-widen scoped policies
conventions: Idempotent DDL, DO-block exception handling for the publication add, create-extension-if-not-exists
Schema deliberately mirrors existing company fabric shapes for clean sync
concerns: Trigger recounts all posts per insert (count(*)) rather than incrementing — O(n) per post, could be costly on hot channels
kind/mode/status/sender_kind are free text enforced only by comment, not CHECK constraints
RLS is enabled with no policies in this file, so the tables are inaccessible to authenticated until 0005/0006 run — correctness depends on migration ordering
notes: The shared channel registry + posts in Supabase as the neutral meeting room between the company fabric and Claude Design, with realtime, a post-count trigger, and RLS enabled but policies deferred to 0005/0006.
questions: Has fork confirmed the provisional schema, or is it still pending adjustment?
Why a full recount in the bump trigger rather than posts = posts + 1?
Are mode/status/sender_kind values validated anywhere outside the comments?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — implements the shared channel meeting ground described in the header (registry + posts + realtime + opt-in sharing), with policies explicitly deferred.
apparent_themes: Supabase as neutral shared room / zero inbound box exposure
intention_signals: Lets Claude Design and the company sessions converse on the same data via an outbound-only shared room, while keeping channels internal by default and only exposing what is explicitly shared and client-scoped.
novelty: The zero-inbound design (both peers reach out to Supabase, neither connects into the box) combined with opt-in two-level channel visibility and origin-tagging to prevent self-echo in the injector.
contribution: Brings the cross-party channel/post data model, the realtime wiring, the post-count trigger, and the RLS-on/policies-deferred posture that make Supabase the shared conversation substrate between the company fabric and Claude Design.
summary_for_embedding: SQL migration creating public.channels and public.channel_posts as a Supabase realtime shared channel substrate (opt-in shared flag, sender_kind tagging, post-count trigger, RLS enabled with policies deferred) mirroring the company channel fabric shapes.
intention_for_embedding: To make Supabase a neutral outbound-only room where the company fabric and Claude Design participate on the same channel data without exposing Tim's box.
